description: Classifies every file in the Cowork Business OS template as engine-owned, user-owned, or seed (installed once, user may edit). Used by the upgrade-workspace skill to decide what to update on upgrade.

engine.description: Template owns these. Overwritten on upgrade without asking. Safe to update because users should not modify them; if they do, they fork.

user.description: User owns these. Never touched on upgrade under any circumstance. Contains all the user's business data, customisations, and content.

exceptions.note: The upgrade skill updates ONLY the top-level `templateVersion` field in this file. All other fields are user-owned.

seed.description: Shipped as starting points for users to customise. On upgrade, if the user has modified the file, the skill presents a diff and asks whether to keep, take new, or show diff. If unmodified since install, upgrade updates it silently.

sample.description: Sample projects and clients shipped for new users. Users choose during setup to delete, keep, or convert to real ones. After setup, these paths behave as user-owned. The upgrade skill never touches anything under Projects/P00* Sample Project/ or Clients/C00* Sample Client/.

precedence: If a path matches multiple classifications, the most specific match wins.
User classification overrides all others — if anything matches a user path pattern, it is never touched.
Sample classification is treated as user-owned after setup; before setup, samples are engine-owned.

Within .claude/skills/, all subfolder files are engine by default — this includes SKILL.md and any files in a references/ subfolder. If a skill has other files the user is expected to edit, they should be listed explicitly under seed.
notes: Any new file added to a future version of the template must be classified here before release.
The upgrade-workspace skill aborts if it encounters a file in the template that is not classified in this manifest — this forces discipline around maintaining the manifest.
A file may be moved between classifications across major versions, but never silently. Any reclassification must be documented in CHANGELOG.md for the release.
The .git/ directory and dotfiles not listed here (e.g. .DS_Store) are ignored — not classified. The skill never touches unclassified dotfiles.
